title: Victoria Street disaster site a no-fly zone
description: GEORGE NEWS - Airports Company South Africa...
bodyText:
Gallery
                            Update

GEORGE NEWS - Airports Company South Africa (Acsa) has declared the disaster site in Victoria Street a no-fly zone.

This follows reports of a private drone flying over the rescue zone yesterday 9 May.


> This restriction will be strictly enforced, and any individuals found violating it will be subject to criminal prosecution.


This morning George Municipality confirmed the death toll to be at 9 after a pasient in critical condition died in hospital.

The total estimated number on-site at the time of incident was 81. 37 people have been retrieved and 44 remain unaccounted for.

The rescue operation is approaching 91 hours, as at 10 May.


> It continues to be a multi-agency approach, involving local excavation contractors, the District Roads Engineer and a large-scale demolition company.


Rescue techniques will still be applied meticulously and sensitively by the highly skilled and experienced disaster management team.


> Photo galleries:


- Evening shift 8 May: Collapsed building
- Collapsed building 8 May: Photo story
- Collapsed building site in Victoria Street, George

The rescue operation is approaching 91 hours, as at 10 May.

‘We bring you the latest Garden Route, Hessequa, Karoo news’
